fields.label: User
fields.description: The username to use for authentication

fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password to use for authentication

relationships.description: HTTP Basic auth credentials sent to the Jenkins API. Only applied when jsonData.username is non-empty (pkg/plugin/datasource.go:66-71); otherwise the client is anonymous.

instructions.msg: Jenkins authentication is HTTP Basic auth or anonymous. The backend applies BasicAuth on the SDK HTTP client only when jsonData.username is non-empty (pkg/plugin/datasource.go:66-71); if jsonData.username is empty the datasource performs anonymous, unauthenticated requests, even if secureJsonData.password is set. There is no auth-type discriminator.

Minimal working payload for Basic auth: {"jsonData":{"url":"https://jenkins.example.com","username":"<user>"},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<password or API token>"}}. The password field accepts either a user password or a Jenkins API token (both are sent verbatim in the Authorization: Basic header). jsonData.url is strictly required — pkg/plugin/settings.go:23-25 returns a DownstreamError "URL is missing" when it is empty. jsonData.username and secureJsonData.password have no backend-side required check.

Minimal working payload for anonymous access: {"jsonData":{"url":"https://jenkins.example.com"}}. Omit jsonData.username (or set it to an empty string) to skip the BasicAuth wiring; the Jenkins client will make unauthenticated requests, which only works against Jenkins instances that permit anonymous read access.

secureJsonData.password is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether the password is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.password (boolean) instead of secureJsonData.password.

jsonData.url is the Jenkins base URL. The client normalizes the value with strings.TrimRight(url, "/") (pkg/jenkins/client.go:226), so a trailing slash is tolerated. API paths (/api/json, /computer/api/json, /queue/api/json, /job/<name>/api/json, /label/<name>/api/json) are joined onto it — do NOT include an API path in the URL.

Legacy datasources may have been provisioned with only jsonData.url (and optionally jsonData.username) and no secureJsonData.password. Nothing in the backend rejects this configuration: an empty password is passed straight into httpclient.BasicAuthOptions when a username is set, and the Jenkins server decides whether the resulting empty-password request is authorised.
